name: Stuffed Leg of Duck with Red Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: Duck, whole (4 to 5 lbs) or 6 duck legs
Turnips (1 lb, white), peeled and cut into spears
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. Debone the duck and reserve the legs for this dish. Discard the rest.
2. In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add bones and cook until browned.
3. Add onion, carrot, tomato (quartered), thyme, bay leaf, peppercorns, and salt.
4. Pour in red wine and veal stock. Simmer for approximately 1 hour.
5. Strain the sauce into a bowl and set aside.
6. In a food processor or fine meat grinder, mix pork, bacon, chicken liver, shallot, egg yolk, cream (3 tbsp), salt, and pepper until fully combined.
7. Peel turnips and cut into spears. Cook in a small amount of water, butter, and salt until tender.
8. When almost all the liquid has evaporated, add 0.5 teaspoon sugar and continue cooking until a brown glaze forms.
9. Deglaze the pan with duck sauce (6 tbsp) from the reserved sauce and set aside.
10. Debone the duck legs, keeping them as one piece. Pound flat using a meat mallet or rolling pin.
11. Place caul fat on a work surface and cut into 5 x 5 inch squares.
12. Stuff each leg square with about 1 tablespoon of the prepared stuffing mixture.
13. Roll up each leg, securing with a toothpick if needed, to form a log shape.
14. Place the logs in a buttered baking dish and cook at 475°F (245°C) for 20-25 minutes.
15. Warm the reserved sauce by heating it gently over low heat.
16. Taste and adjust seasoning as necessary.
17. To serve, slice the duck legs, arrange them on plates, garnish with turnips, and pour any remaining sauce over the top.
